Project.name: ocserv-admin-mcp
Project.annotation: A generic safe administrative package for `ocserv` that lets an operator manage VPN users, policy groups, and active sessions without manually editing server configuration. It exposes a constrained MCP tool surface and localhost backend for safe execution on the VPS, and can be integrated into Nanobot or any other MCP-capable agent.
Project.keywords: ocserv, openconnect, anyconnect, mcp, vpn-admin, access-control, policy-groups, infrastructure-automation, audit-logging, agent-integration

Actors.actor-Operator: Authorized human administrator using an approved MCP-capable client and the constrained tool surface to manage VPN users, groups, and sessions.
Actors.actor-System: Constrained backend components that validate, execute, and audit approved administrative actions.
Actors.actor-Ocserv: Target VPN service and control surface exposed through ocserv, occtl, systemctl, and config files.

DM-USER.definition: VPN identity managed by the system and represented as the unit of create, disable, remove, and group-assignment operations.
DM-USER.mvp-behavior: Usernames identify ocserv-facing access subjects, not operators, devices, or overlay-network objects.

DM-POLICY-GROUP.definition: ocserv policy group that controls access rules and routing policy applied to a VPN user.
DM-POLICY-GROUP.mvp-behavior: Group always means ocserv policy group and never a separate L3 network or tenant boundary.

DM-SESSION.definition: Observed active VPN connection state returned by occtl for a connected VPN identity.
DM-SESSION.mvp-behavior: Session operations act on real occtl-visible runtime state and do not imply persistent user configuration changes.

DM-COMMAND-AUDIT-ENTRY.definition: Structured audit record describing operator intent, approved action, target object, execution outcome, and failure boundary when present.
DM-COMMAND-AUDIT-ENTRY.mvp-behavior: Audit entries capture command intent and result without exposing secrets, credentials, or full config payloads.

MvpAccessModel.decision-2: Group means ocserv policy group, not a separate L3 network.
MvpAccessModel.decision-3: Network group in MVP means a named set of access rules or routes, not a distinct VPN overlay.
MvpAccessModel.decision-4: LAN-behind-client operations are deferred and remain out of MVP scope.

tool-list_users.PURPOSE: List managed VPN identities for operator inspection

tool-list_groups.PURPOSE: List configured ocserv policy groups and membership evidence

tool-show_user_ips.PURPOSE: Show static IP assignments for managed VPN identities

tool-disconnect_session.PURPOSE: Disconnect an active ocserv session through a destructive confirmed action

tool-create_user.PURPOSE: Create a VPN identity and optionally associate the intended policy group

tool-update_user_ip.PURPOSE: Update a managed VPN identity static IPv4 assignment

tool-disable_user.PURPOSE: Disable a VPN identity without silently deleting it

tool-disable_group_users.PURPOSE: Disable all managed VPN identities assigned to an ocserv policy group through a destructive confirmed action

tool-delete_user.PURPOSE: Remove a VPN identity through a destructive confirmed action

tool-assign_group.PURPOSE: Assign an existing VPN identity to an ocserv policy group

tool-create_group.PURPOSE: Create an ocserv policy group record in the managed configuration scope

tool-delete_group.PURPOSE: Delete an empty ocserv policy group record through a destructive confirmed action

tool-reload_service.PURPOSE: Validate config and reload ocserv safely

tool-rollback_last_change.PURPOSE: Restore the last config-affecting change through a destructive confirmed action

tool-confirm_action.PURPOSE: Confirm or cancel a pending destructive backend action token

UC-001.Action: Create a VPN user and assign an ocserv policy group
UC-001.Goal: Provision access safely without manually editing server configuration
UC-001.Preconditions: Operator is authorized, target identity is valid, requested group exists, and backend can reach required ocserv control surfaces.
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: User creation is recorded in the audit log, the intended group assignment is applied, config validation passes, reload is safe, the user is visible afterward, and the operator receives a usable one-time credential or equivalent secure provisioning handoff.

UC-002.Action: Disable or remove a VPN user
UC-002.Goal: Perform destructive administration safely and on the correct identity
UC-002.Preconditions: Operator is authorized, the target identity is resolved unambiguously, and a confirmation step has completed.
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Destructive action requires confirmation, the wrong identity is not modified, the final result is auditable, and failure states are surfaced clearly.

UC-003.Action: Inspect active sessions and disconnect an active session
UC-003.Goal: Provide real VPN session visibility and controlled active-session termination through the constrained operator surface.
UC-003.Preconditions: Operator is authorized and active session information is available from occtl.
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Real sessions are listed accurately, session disconnect requires confirmation, the correct VPN identity is targeted, and the result is auditable when routed through the controlled operator surface.

UC-004.Action: Manage ocserv policy groups and assign users to groups
UC-004.Goal: Apply intended access policy and keep policy-group records aligned without corrupting unrelated configuration
UC-004.Preconditions: Operator is authorized, target user exists, target group exists, and policy files are writable in the approved scope.
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Group listing reflects managed policy groups, group creation is scoped to the managed configuration, group deletion refuses unsafe non-empty deletion, user assignment updates only the intended identity, validation passes, and every change is auditable.

UC-005.Action: Validate config and reload ocserv safely
UC-005.Goal: Preserve service availability and create a clear rollback-oriented audit trail when reload fails
UC-005.Preconditions: A configuration change is staged and the backend can run validation and reload commands safely.
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: If validation or reload fails, execution stops, service availability is preserved where possible, and the audit record contains the intent, failure point, and outcome.

UC-006.Action: Inspect and update managed user static IP assignments
UC-006.Goal: Keep per-user VPN IP allocation visible and correct through the constrained operator surface.
UC-006.Preconditions: Operator is authorized, target user exists for mutation, and requested IPv4 assignment is syntactically valid.
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: User IP listings show managed assignments without exposing secrets, IP updates reject invalid addresses, only the intended user record changes, validation passes, and the result is auditable.

UC-007.Action: Disable all users assigned to an ocserv policy group
UC-007.Goal: Perform bulk access suspension safely for a policy group without deleting identities or mutating unrelated groups.
UC-007.Preconditions: Operator is authorized, target group resolves unambiguously, and the destructive confirmation flow has completed.
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Bulk disable requires confirmation, only users in the target group are disabled, identities are not deleted, unaffected groups remain unchanged, and the action emits auditable per-operation evidence.

NonGoals.item-1: Route orchestration is out of MVP.
NonGoals.item-2: LAN-behind-client publishing is out of MVP.
NonGoals.item-3: Firewall automation is out of MVP.
NonGoals.item-4: General-purpose infrastructure control outside user, session, and group administration is out of scope.

Constraints.constraint-1: The system must run on a Linux VPS under systemd and interact safely with ocserv, occtl, systemctl, and sudo-mediated operations.
Constraints.constraint-2: The package exposes only the approved MCP tools and localhost backend; Telegram, chat UX, and agent orchestration remain outside the repository boundary.
Constraints.constraint-7: Nanobot is one supported integration example, not a required runtime dependency of the package boundary.
Constraints.constraint-3: Secrets, bot tokens, passwords, certificate material, and session cookies must never appear in logs.
Constraints.constraint-4: Group means ocserv policy group, not a separate overlay network abstraction.
Constraints.constraint-5: Initial scope is limited to user, session, and policy-group administration.
Constraints.constraint-6: Any network-group wording in MVP must be interpreted as access-rule or route grouping, not as a separate VPN topology layer.

Risks.risk-1: Destructive identity operations may target the wrong user if name resolution and confirmation flows are weak.
Risks.risk-2: Policy-group changes may corrupt unrelated config if file writes are not tightly scoped.
Risks.risk-3: Reload failures can degrade service availability unless validation and stop-on-failure behavior are enforced.
Risks.risk-4: Session listings can drift from real occtl state if output parsing is brittle or stale.
Risks.risk-5: Overly broad orchestration could exceed the constrained execution model intended for safe VPS administration.

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should the localhost admin API remain private to MCP clients only, or also support a separate authenticated operator client later?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Which exact ocserv config files and policy file locations are canonical in the target deployment?
OpenQuestions.question-3: Should user provisioning integrate with certificates, password files, or an external identity source in later phases?
